sound_type: clucking + grunting flock -- continuous varied stream of clucking, grunting, cackling from social flocks on open water; E=4
ecosystem: Open water -- lakes, ponds, slow rivers, reservoirs, park ponds. Swims on open water. Year-round resident.
season: Year-round resident. Flocks largest in winter. Vocalizations year-round.

coordinates.description: American Coot on lakes, ponds, and reservoirs throughout Puget Sound lowlands. Common and conspicuous.

taxonomy_note.description: THIRD RALLIDAE. Cluster complete (161-162, 164). Looks like a duck but is a rail. White bill, lobed toes, head-bobbing swim. Most frequently misidentified waterbird. 'Not a duck.'
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Rallidae: third and final in cluster. Virginia Rail (161) + Sora (162) + American Coot (164).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Fulica from Latin 'fulica' (coot). americana for American.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Fulica -- the coots. Open-water rails with lobed toes. Worldwide.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Third Rallidae. Cluster complete. The visible rail. 'Not a duck.'

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 500-5000 Hz across repertoire. Clucks lower (500-2000 Hz), cackles higher (2000-5000 Hz).
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Continuous flock sound. Individual calls: 0.1-0.5 seconds. Aggregate: unbroken while active.
song_metrics.note_structure: Multiple call types: clucks ('kuk-kuk-kuk'), grunts ('unh-unh'), cackles ('ka-ka-ka-ka'), pips (sharp single notes). Continuous stream.
song_metrics.note_types: Flock chatter (primary), territorial aggression 'KA-KA-KA-KA!' (loud, harsh), night calls (soft grunts/pips).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: No structured phrase. Continuous stream of varied calls from social flock. Individual calls brief, aggregate unbroken.
song_metrics.timbre: Varied. Clucks dry and sharp. Grunts nasal. Cackles harsh. The flock sound is a busy, chattering ambiance.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Individual calls moderate. Flock aggregate substantial. Aggression calls significantly louder.
song_metrics.tempo: Rapid, overlapping. Multiple birds calling simultaneously.
song_metrics.context: Social interaction. Flock cohesion. Feeding. Territorial aggression. Year-round.
song_metrics.key_property: CLUCKING + GRUNTING FLOCK -- continuous social vocalization. THIRD RALLIDAE. 'Not a duck.' White bill, lobed toes. Aggressive foot-fighting. E=4. v1.49.304.

plumage.overall: Black body. White bill with dark subterminal band and reddish frontal shield. Green legs.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: White chicken-like bill (NOT flat like a duck). Black body. Head-bobbing swim. Lobed toes visible when walking.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Frontal shield slightly redder in breeding season.
plumage.flight_pattern: Labored takeoff with running start across water. Stronger in sustained flight. Feet trail behind.

physical_description.overall: Length: 34-43 cm. Weight: 430-850 g. Duck-sized. Stocky.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: White bill. Black body. Green legs. Lobed toes. Head-bobbing swim.
physical_description.field_id: On open water in flocks. Looks like a duck but: white bill (not flat), head bobs while swimming, lobed toes (not webbed). If it has a white bill and bobs its head, it's a coot, not a duck.

nodes.title: Flock Vocalization
nodes.description: Continuous clucking, grunting, cackling stream from social flocks. 500-5000 Hz. Aggregate of 50-500+ birds. Unmistakable ambient sound of any pond with coots.

nodes.title: Not a Duck
nodes.description: Rallidae, not Anatidae. White chicken-like bill, lobed toes, head-bobbing swim. Most frequently misidentified waterbird in N.A. A rail in duck habitat.

nodes.title: Lobed Toes
nodes.description: Scalloped flaps on each toe. Not webbed. Provides swimming thrust + efficient land walking + diving. Rail foot plan modified for open water. More versatile than duck webbing.

nodes.title: Aggressive Territorial Behavior
nodes.description: Foot-fighting with lobed toes as weapons. Rearing, striking, locking feet, pushing across water. Brood parasitism defense. Egg counting. Flock dominance hierarchy.

nodes.title: Rallidae Cluster Complete (161-162, 164)
nodes.description: Three entries. Virginia Rail (invisible skulker), Sora (invisible short-billed), American Coot (visible swimmer). All E=4. From total secrecy to full visibility. One family, full behavioral range.

cross_references.relationship: RALLIDAE RANGE! Virginia Rail: invisible skulker. Coot: visible swimmer. Same family, opposite strategies.
RALLIDAE TRIO! Virginia Rail (161), Sora (162), American Coot (164). Three entries, three strategies, same family.
MARSH TO LAKE! Bittern: dense marsh, invisible. Coot: open water, visible. Adjacent degrees, opposite visibility.
